info.name: Kyros API
info.description:
Generated from backend/openapi.json — regenerate with `make openapi && make postman`.

Quick start: run the '00 — Demo & Smoke' folder against a seeded dev backend (`make bootstrap` or `make seed`). Logging in via any auth request auto-captures {{accessToken}}/{{refreshToken}}.

Demo accounts (synthetic dev fixtures, password `DemoPass123!`): patients +919700000001..05, doctors +919800000001..03, coordinator +919800000010. None of these exist in production.

Every request asserts status < 500, and every /v1 response is checked for `Cache-Control: no-store` (PHI cache protection).

exec: pm.test('PHI cache protection: Cache-Control is no-store', () => {

item.name: 00 — Demo & Smoke (seeded dev data)
item.description:
Run top-to-bottom with the Collection Runner against a dev backend after `make seed`. Demo accounts (synthetic, dev-only):

| Role | Phone | Email |
|---|---|---|
| Patient | +919700000001…05 | sunita.reddy@dev.kyros.local … |
| Doctor | +919800000001…03 | dr.meera@dev.kyros.local … |
| Coordinator | +919800000010 | ananya.menon@dev.kyros.local |

Password for all: `DemoPass123!`. With KYROS_DEBUG=true the signup response includes `otp_hint`, so OTP flows are testable without SMS.

OTP delivery chain: WhatsApp → email → SMS. In dev the email lands in MailHog ({{mailhogUrl}}); the 'Email OTP 1/3..3/3' requests run the whole flow hands-free.

exec: pm.test('login as demo patient succeeded', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: Email OTP 1/3 — request OTP via email channel

request.description: `channel: email` asks for email delivery first (WhatsApp/SMS remain as fallback). The code lands in MailHog in dev — run 2/3 next to fetch it automatically. Omit `channel` for the default WhatsApp-first chain. Note the 60s per-phone resend cooldown (429 otp_cooldown).

item.name: Email OTP 2/3 — fetch code from MailHog (dev inbox)

request.description: Reads the demo patient's inbox from MailHog (the dev SMTP server, UI at {{mailhogUrl}}) and extracts the 6-digit code from the latest 'Your Kyros verification code' email into the {{otp}} variable. Dev-only — production uses real SMTP.

exec: pm.test('an OTP email was delivered', () => pm.expect(data.total).to.be.above(0));
// MailHog stores raw MIME: undo quoted-printable soft line breaks, then
// pull the code out of the HTML body (rendered as >123456<).

item.name: Email OTP 3/3 — verify code (logs in as patient)

request.description: Verifies the emailed code through the same path as WhatsApp/SMS codes (one Redis-stored HMAC per phone) and returns tokens. Max 5 attempts per code; the code expires after 5 minutes.

exec: pm.test('login as demo patient (via email OTP) succeeded', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: Overwrites {{accessToken}} — subsequent requests run as the doctor.

exec: pm.test('login as demo doctor succeeded', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: Overwrites {{accessToken}} — subsequent requests run as the coordinator.

exec: pm.test('login as demo coordinator succeeded', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: Refresh tokens (rotation — old refresh token is revoked)

exec: pm.test('login as demo session refresh succeeded', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description:
Signup

OTP delivery: the optional `channel` field ('whatsapp' | 'email' | 'sms') selects which channel is tried first; omit it for the default order WhatsApp → email → SMS. The remaining channels stay as fallback if the chosen one fails. `channel: email` returns 422 email_channel_unavailable if the account has no registered email (or the email fallback is disabled via KYROS_OTP_EMAIL_FALLBACK_ENABLED). All channels deliver the same 6-digit code; verification is identical regardless of channel. In local dev emailed codes land in MailHog ({{mailhogUrl}}). With KYROS_DEBUG=true the response also includes otp_hint.

exec: const data = pm.response.json ? (() => { try { return pm.response.json(); } catch { return {}; } })() : {};

request.description:
Send Otp

OTP delivery: the optional `channel` field ('whatsapp' | 'email' | 'sms') selects which channel is tried first; omit it for the default order WhatsApp → email → SMS. The remaining channels stay as fallback if the chosen one fails. `channel: email` returns 422 email_channel_unavailable if the account has no registered email (or the email fallback is disabled via KYROS_OTP_EMAIL_FALLBACK_ENABLED). All channels deliver the same 6-digit code; verification is identical regardless of channel. In local dev emailed codes land in MailHog ({{mailhogUrl}}).

exec: const data = pm.response.json ? (() => { try { return pm.response.json(); } catch { return {}; } })() : {};

request.description:
Verify Otp

Verifies the code regardless of which channel delivered it (WhatsApp, email, or SMS) — single Redis-stored HMAC per phone.

exec: const data = pm.response.json ? (() => { try { return pm.response.json(); } catch { return {}; } })() : {};
const data = pm.response.json ? (() => { try { return pm.response.json(); } catch { return {}; } })() : {};
const data = pm.response.json ? (() => { try { return pm.response.json(); } catch { return {}; } })() : {};

request.description:
Publish content with doctor approval.

The approving user must be a doctor (super_admin role with a dr_doctors row).
Stores reviewed_by_doctor_id for NMC TPG compliance audit trail.
Mark an intake consultation as confirmed and optionally add coordinator notes.
Escalate emergency intake to super admin.
Send a pre-approved template message via WhatsApp or email.

Annotate a patient's lab report with commentary and/or attention flags.

The report must belong to a patient on the doctor's panel.
Body may contain `commentary` (dict biomarker→text) and/or
`patient_attention_flags` (list of biomarker names).
At least one field must be provided.
Enqueue an on-demand report generation task.  Returns immediately with task_id.

Submit bank account details for revenue share.

Details are encrypted at rest; a verification alert is sent to super admin.
Allow patient to correct OCR-parsed biomarker data.
Return the seven Kyros clinical verticals for display on the public website.

Submit a pre-account booking inquiry from the public website booking flow.

No authentication required. A care coordinator will follow up within 4 hours.
Intake responses are stored as JSONB — no PHI beyond name and contact details.
Register or update the Expo push token for push notification delivery.
